title: gateway: inject BLE UART IPv6 packets into mesh
description: Feature-completeness gap found during Zephyr firmware status audit. The BLE UART path currently accepts IPv6-looking bytes but does not inject them into the Zephyr net_pkt/L2 path or mesh; it only logs that mesh injection is deferred. This leaves the Local Client Interface/BLE ingress incomplete. Evidence: lichen/apps/gateway/src/ble_uart.c:80 has TODO to inject the IPv6 packet into the mesh via Zephyr net_pkt and logs 'mesh injection deferred' at :84. Acceptance: convert valid BLE UART IPv6 frames into the same ingress path used by local/network packets, preserve ownership/lifetime rules for net_pkt buffers, reject malformed packets with useful logs, and add focused tests or a native_sim scenario proving a BLE-originated IPv6 packet reaches the mesh/L2 path.
acceptance_criteria: After implementation, run the relevant tests/builds and then complete two separate code-review passes before closing. Each review pass should inspect the produced code against this Bead's acceptance criteria and file follow-up Beads for any discovered issues.
